title: Time Capsule
description: 3D first-person puzzle game where you freeze physics objects mid-air to build platforms and collect artifacts in a museum — built in Godot 4.2 with AI-generated 3D models via Meshy.
overview: A 3D first-person puzzle platformer built in Godot 4.2 for a graduate IoT & Game Dev course. Players explore a museum and must collect 5 artifacts within a 3-minute time limit, using a freeze ray mechanic to suspend physics objects mid-air and construct makeshift platforms to reach otherwise inaccessible areas.

sections.heading: Core Mechanics
bullets: Freeze Ray System: Raycast-based mechanic that suspends RigidBody3D objects in mid-air for 10 seconds — up to 3 active freeze charges at a time, with per-object countdown timers and automatic unfreeze logic written in GDScript
First-Person Controller: Full WASD movement, mouse-look with clamped vertical rotation, jump with gravity, and smooth velocity falloff
Game State Management: Dedicated GameManager node handles 3-minute countdown, win/lose conditions, UI signals, and scene tree pause on game end
HUD System: Live timer with color-change warnings, freeze charge counter, artifact progress tracker, crosshair, and distinct win/lose overlay screens
AI-Generated 3D Assets: Museum artifacts and environment props created with Meshy AI (text-to-3D), exported as .glb and imported directly into Godot

sections.heading: Architecture
bullets: 7 GDScript files with clean separation: player controller, artifact logic, freeze orb pickups, freezable physics objects, game manager, UI manager, and menu
6 scene files (.tscn) for player, artifact, freeze orb, physics object, main level, and HUD
Collision layer system — players on layer 1, freezable objects on layer 2; raycast mask scoped to layer 2 for precise object targeting
